heading: Reports to the Office of Personnel Management
label: 2951 Reports to the Office of Personnel Management
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large 8 (less last sentence).5 U.S.C. 633(2)8 (less last sentence), .Jan. 16, 1883, ch. 27, § 2(2)22 Stat. 404The authority of the President to prescribe rules is added on authority of former section 633(1), which is carried into section 3302.In paragraph (1), the word “authority” is substituted for “power”. The words “or employment” are omitted as included within “appointment”.In paragraph (1)(B), the words “separation during probation” are substituted for “of the rejection of any such person after probation”. The words “rejection . . . after probation” refer to a rejection, i.e., separation, after a portion of the probationary period has been served but before the end of the probationary period. This is so because an individual can be rejected only during the probationary period. After he has completed the probationary period, he can be removed only under procedures governing removals from the competitive service, and removals of this nature are covered by paragraph (E).Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1978—, substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission” in section catchline.Pub. L. 95–454, § 906(a)(16)Pars. (1), (2). substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Office” for “Commission”.Pub. L. 95–454
Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 95–454section 907 of Pub. L. 95–454section 1101 of this titleOct. 13, 1978
